entries.title: Job the righteous man washed in the water of the Tekaze and was healed of all his sickness
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Job the righteous man washed in the water of the Tekaze and was healed of all his sickness. It also remembers the repose of the holy Apostle Bartholomew, who was one of the Twelve Apostles. diminisheth because this month is the first of the circle of the years of Egypt and Ethiopia.

Behold old works have passed away, and behold new work sare known, and every work is from God" .

entries.title: The holy father
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father, Abba Macarius, Archbishop of Alexandria; now he was the sixty-ninth archbishop of the city. This father was an ascetic and practiced strenuous self-denial from his earliest years, and he craved for the garb of monasticism. He went up to the desert of Scete and became a monk in the church of Abba Macarius, and he perf orm edgreat and severe ascetic labors.

entries.readMore: This father was an ascetic and practiced strenuous self-denial from his earliest years, and he craved for the garb of monasticism. He went up to the desert of Scete and became a monk in the church of Abba Macarius, and he perf orm edgreat and severe ascetic labors. He illum ined his soul by reading the Holy Scriptures and translations of them, and he learned the explanations of them. He excelled in the working of right eousness, and he was appointed priest in the desert of Scete.

entries.readMore: After this she cam eto the Bishop of Manufe (i.e. Manuf Al-'Alia, or Panouf Res, capital of the province of Manufiayyah) and believed in Christ, and said unto him, "I have believed in thy God." And after this he taught her all the Law of the Church, and he baptized her and her daughters in the Nam eof the Father and the Son and the Holy Ghost. Thereupon they laid an accusation against her before Kaldeyanos , the governor of the city, saying that she had becom e a Christian and ha dforsaken the worship of idols. the governor questioned her closely and she did not deny it, and he condem ned her and her daughters to be punished severely.

entries.title: The great Prophet Isaiah
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the great Prophet Isaiah, the son of 'Amos, Manasseh having sawn him in twain with a wood saw. It also remembers the holy woman Baslantya in the days of Diocletian, the em peror who denied [Christ]. This prophet prophesied in the days of the reigns of five kings, that is to say Uziah, and Jotham, and Ahaz, and Hezekiah his son, and Manasseh the son of Hezekiah.

entries.title: Saint Zacharias the priest
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Zacharias the priest, the son of Berachiah, was martyred by the hand of Herod the king. It also remembers the repose of Moses the prophet and righteous man, and head of the Prophets. When the angel Gabriel announced to this priest the birth of John his son, he did not believe his word.

entries.title: Fasiladas
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Fasiladas, the father of the kings of Antioch and their counselor, became a martyr. It also remembers the holy woman Banaf zez [literally "violet"], the interpretation whereof is, "W hose odor is sweet," became a martyr. This holy man was a general of the kings of Rom, and the whole kingdom stood by reason of his counsel; and he had many slaves and handm aidens.

entries.title: Archangel Michael the archangel
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the glorious angel Michael the archangel. God commanded him to go to Hezekiah the king, and to tell him that God would heal him of his sickness, and had a dded fifteen years to his days, so that he might m a rry and beget Manasseh. This was the third of the great General Councils, and its bishops assem bled in the twentieth year of the reign of Theodosius the Less, and son of Arcadius, the son of Thodosius the Great.

entries.title: The miracle which the great Saint Abba Basil
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the miracle which the great Saint Abba Basil, Bishop of Caesarea and Cappadocia, wrought in connecti on with the young man who lusted for his mas'sdaughter, and whose heart was inflam ed with love and desire for her. It also remembers Abba Isaac the anchorite became a martyr. that young man went to Satan, the enem y of our race, by the help of an unbeliever and m a gician.

entries.readMore: This holy man came from the city of Tenis (Tanis, or the Fi eld of Zoan, i.e. San Al-Hagar) in the north of the land of Egypt. His parents were righteous folk, and fearers of God, and they loved to give alm sand gifts to the poor and needy. The thoughts of his mind tended to monasticism, and they moved in his heart continually.

entries.readMore: why did this great hill com einto being? It wa sbecause of the many signs and wonders, which were made man ifest at the holy tom bof our Lord Jesus Christ; for the dead were raised, and the paralytics were healed, and the sick were made whole. because of these things the Jews were furiously angry, and they sent forth a decree throughout all the country of Judea and Jerusalem ordering that every man should cast the sweepings of his house, and the ashes, and offal of every kind on the grave of our Lord Jesus Christ. the Jews did this for more than two hundred years, and the ashes and the offal form ed a very great heap, and they did so until the Em press Helena cam eto Jerusalem.

Then he took him to the monastery of Abba Zacharias, his mother'sbrother, who when he saw him knew that the grace of God was dwelling upon him, and he taught him all the rules of monas ticism, and he arrayed him in the apparel of the angels.

entries.title: Saint Gregory
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Gregory, Archbishop of the country of Arm enia, who became a martyr without the shedding of his blood. It also remembers is celebrated the festival of the Holy and Honorable Cross, for when it was found the people made a festival for three days with joy and gladness. This man established himself in the country of Arm enia in the days of Dertades the king.

Now that king was an infidel, and when he cam einto his tem ple to offer up incense to the gods, he sum moned Gregory to offer up incense to his gods likewise; but Gregory would not obey the king'scom man d, and he tortured him with exceedingly severe tortures, and had him burned with fire. After this he cast him into a pit naked, and he lived therein for fifteen years. there was a certain old wom ana widow, near the king'spa lace, and she saw a vi sion wherein one said unto her, "Make bread regularly, and cast it into the pit wherein the holy man is." entries.readMore: This father was the steward of the church of the city of Alexandria. When Pe the archbishop, his predecessor, died, all the bishops, and the governors , and the elders, and the people, with one consent agreed to appoint Saint Athanasius archbishop, for the holiness of his life was prove rbial, and he contended splendidly in the way of God, and the Holy Spir it dwelt in him. they took him against his will and appointed him archbishop, and he protected th eflock of our Lord Jesus Christ well and carefully against the wolves of the devil, who are wicked Satans, by means of his adm onitions, and teachings and prayers. He sat as archbishop seven years, and he was well pleasing to God, and he died in peace.

entries.readMore: These holy menwere the sons of Sapor, King of Pe rsia, and they had a companion whose nam ewas Tatos. Now Sapor, King of Persia, worshi pped fire and the sun, and he afflicted and tortured many believing men, and no man thr oughout his country dared to mention the Nam eof Christ. his son Kotolos had a friend whose nam ewas Tatos, and he was governor of the country of the Mayda swiyan; and certain people laid inform ation against him before Sapor the king, and told him that he was a Christian. the king sent to him a certain noble whose nam ewas Tum a ge rto find out whether that which was said about Tatos was true or not, and he com man de dthat noble, if he found that Tatos was a Christian, to inflict heavy punishm ent upon him.

entries.title: The holy father
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father, Abba Gregory the monk. It also remembers the repose of Saint Bedratos, one of the two and seventy disciples whom our Lord Jesus Christ chose. This holy and blessed man was a native of Upper Egypt, and his parents were holy and righteous Christians, and they were exceedingly rich, and their possessions were very great.

entries.readMore: This holy and blessed man was a native of Upper Egypt, and his parents were holy and righteous Christians, and they were exceedingly rich, and their possessions were very great. they taught their son every kind of sacred and profane learning, and then they taught him the doctrine of wisdom and the art of speaking, and the Books of the Holy C hurch. Next they sent him to Father Isaac, bishop of their city, and he laid his hand upon him and made him a reader; then they required from him that he should m a rry a wife, but this thing he refused [to do]. After this they brought him to the bishop, and he appoint ed him a full deacon.

entries.title: God sent the angel Gabriel to Zacharias the priest
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates God sent the angel Gabriel to Zacharias the priest, the son of Barachias, and told him concerning John the Baptist, his son. It also remembers the tran slation of the body of 'Aboli the martyr, the son of Justus. Now Zacharias was a righteous man, and he was exceedingly old, and his period for begetting children had passed; moreover, his wife Elisabeth was childless, and her period for conceiving and bringing forth children was passed.

entries.readMore: This man was one of the chief generals of the Im perial Ar m y of Rome, and he knew not God. His own original nam ewas Kudos, and he gave alm sin abundance, and expended much charity on the poor and needy, and God did not wish that his toil should be in vain. Eustathius was in the desert hunting wild anim als, and one day there appeared unto him the figure of a cross between the horns of a stag, and the cr oss-reached up to the clouds. Eustathius chased the stag into the mountains, and he pur sued it that he might kill it.

Now 'Abadir succeeded his father as com man der-in-chief, and he had ama nsion wherein he used to pray. our Lord Jesus Christ appeared unto him at midnight, and He said unto him, "Rise up, and take thy sister 'Ira'Iand get thee in to the land of Egypt so that thou m a yest receive a crown of martyrdom. I will co mma nd a certain man whose nam eis Samuel to take care about your bodies, and he shall pr epare them for burial"; and He gave him a benediction and went up into heaven with great glory.

They brought her up in all honor, and trained her in ama nner befitting her nobl erank, and they taught her everything, which was beneficial for her soul and body, and also the doctrine of the Church. When she arrived at woman'sestate her parents wanted to give her in m a rriage, but she did not desire this thing, for from her youth up she had craved for the garb of the ascetic life; and she chose the spiritual fight which is the garb of the holy ascetic lif e. she entered one of the nunneries where virgins were received in the city of Rome, and she put on the nun'sgarb, which is the garb of ange ls.

entries.title: The great Saint Abba Severus

entries.title: Abba Sim eon
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father Abba Sim eon, the fifty-first archbishop of the city of Alexandria. It also remembers the new Saint George became a martyr. This father was the son of one of the elders of the city of Alexandria, and his parents were Orthodox Christians.

From his earliest childhood he sucked the milk of the Orthodox Faith, and he learned the doctrine of the Church. he dem ined within himself to assum ethe monastic garb, and he went out from the city of Alexandria and departed to the desert of Scete, and he became a monk in the monastery of Abba Jacob, who was archbishop before him. he dwelt with him many days, and he made his body to be em aciated through the strenuousness of hi sascetic exercises, and by his excessive contending.

God protected the body of Saint Bacchus, and brought it through the sea to the coast.

entries.title: Abba Pawli of the city of Tam a w
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father Abba Pawli of the city of Tam a w. It also remembers Minas, and Hasina [and Cyprian, and Justina] became martyrs. This holy man became a monk and a fighter in his early years, and he dwelt in Debra 'Ensena [near Asna] in Upper Egypt, and his disciple Ezekiel was with him, and this man became a witness to the excellences of his m a ster, and to his asce tic com bats.

entries.readMore: This holy man became a monk and a fighter in his early years, and he dwelt in Debra 'Ensena [near Asna] in Upper Egypt, and his disciple Ezekiel was with him, and this man became a witness to the excellences of his m a ster, and to his asce tic com bats. This holy man Abba Pawli contended strenuously because of his love for Christ, and he killed himself seven tim es. The first time he hung himself upon a tree head downwards, and he remained hanging there for forty days and forty nights until his blood flowed from his nose, and he yielded up his soul. God raised him up from death by His power.

When Decius the infidel reigned he established the worship of idols, and afflicted all Christian people, and his Edict cam eto the city of Alex andria, and he afflicted all the people thereof and poured out their blood. Saint Metra wa saccused of being a Christian before the governor, and the governor had Saint Metra brought before him, and he held the m a tto be serious. Saint Metra confessed our Lord Jesus Christ, and proclaim ed that He was God.

entries.title: The pure and righteous woman Hannah
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the pure and righteous woman Hannah, the mother of our Lady, the holy Virgin Mary, the God-bearer. It also remembers Saint Archelaus the martyr, and Elisa the abbot, and Saint Pachom ius, and Minas the martyr, and his mother Urania, Salutation to Archelaus, Elisa, and Pachom ius. This holy woman was a native of the city of Jerusalem, and she was the daugh of Matat, the son of Levi, the son of Melki, of the sons of Aaron the priest, and of the tribe of Levi.

Matat had three children, and the nam eof the eldest was Mary; the nam eof the second was Sofia, and the nam eof the third was Hannah. Mary married and brought forth Salom e, the midwife who received our Lady Mary when she brought forth Christ our God. Sofi ama rried and brought forth Elisabeth, the mother of John the Baptist, and this holy woman Hannah married the righteous man Joachim, who was of the tribe of Judah, and she brought forth our Lady Mary.

entries.title: Is celebrated the com mem orative feast of the glorious angel
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates is celebrated the com mem orative feast of the glorious angel, Michael the archangel. Samuel went to Jesse, and he said unto him, "Bring hither all thy sons": and Jesse brought them [all] with the exception of David, who was tending the sheep in the fields. When Samuel raised the horn of oil of kings over the heads of the sons of Jesse, [he saw that ] God was not pleased with them.

Samuel said unto Jesse, "D oth there not rem a in unto thee som eother son? " And Jesse said unto him, "There re m a ineth the youth, the youngest of them, who is herding the sheep in the fields." And Samuel said unto him, "Bring him hither"; and Jesse brought him.

entries.title: The holy father
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father, Theophilus, the twenty-third Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. It also remembers the repose of Rom a nus the martyr in the days of Asclepianus, the governor who persecuted the Christians. He was appointed in the sixth year of the reign of Theodosius the Great.

He was appointed Archbishop of th ecity of Constantinopl e, when the days of this father were twenty-eight years, and he died in exile. in the days of this father there lived also Epipahanius, Bishop of Cyprus; and in his days also died Abba Cyril, Bishop of Jerusalem; and in his days also ro se up the seven children af they had been asleep for three hundred and sevent y-two years.

Salutation to Bartholomew and his wife who were bur ied alive [in the days of the Emperor Diocletian].

entries.title: The great father of light
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the great father of light, Abba John the Short. This holy man was a native of the city, which is called Tansa and is in Upper Egypt, and he had one brother. His parents were righteous people, and they feared God; they were not rich in the goods and possessions of this world, but they were ri ch in good works.

And he wished to put on the garb of the monk. he found a certain tried and strong old man, whose nam ewas Abba Bam oy, of the city of Seha, dwelling in Scet e, and Saint John bowed low before him and entreated him that he might dwell with him in Scete.

entries.title: Saint Luke the evangelist
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Luke the evangelist, the physician, became a martyr. This holy man was one of the num ber of the seventy-two discip les whom the Holy Gospel mentioned; he minised to the Apostles Peter and Paul and he wrote the histories of them. After the death of Peter and Paul, Luke preached in the country of Rome.

entries.title: Saint Abba Joseph
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Saint Abba Joseph, the fift y-second Archbishop of Alexandria. It also remembers Saint Dionysius, Bishop of the city of Corinth, became a martyr in the days of Diocletian and Maxim ianus, the wicked em perors. This man was the son of one of the elders of the city of Manuf, and he had very many possessions.

entries.readMore: This man was the son of one of the elders of the city of Manuf, and he had very many possessions. When his parents died and left him an or phan a certain God-fearing man brought him up, and when he arrived at man'sestate he took his goods and gave them to the poor and the beggars. Then he went up into the desert of Scete, to the monasy of Saint Abba Macarius, and he became a monk under the dir ection of a certain holy old man. When Abba Mark the second was appointed archbishop, and he heard that the natural disposition and intelligence of this Saint Abba Joseph were good, he sent and had him brought unto him, and established him in his house.

entries.title: The fighter Abba Ablarius the monk and anchorite

entries.readMore: In this holy man were fulfilled the words of Da vid the prophet, saying, "Blessed is the man who hath not walked in the counsel of the ungodly, who hath not stood in the way of sinners, and who hath not sat in the seat of th escoffers, but whose pleasure is in the Law of God, and in that Law he doth meditate day and night" . This holy man kept the com man dm ent of God, and he traded with [his] talent and gained profit thereby. How many were the signs and great wonders, which God wrought by the hands of this holy man! Concerning him it is said that when he was in the city of Kaw, every time he took his seat upon the throne to teach the people he would weep continuously.

These holy menwere disciples of Saint Paul the martyr, Archbi shop of the city of C onstantine. saints Marcianus and Mercurius wrote jointly on the day of the death of Saint Paul, and cursed the em peror, and they said of him that he believed the faith of Arius the inf idel, and they anathem a tized Arius and the em peror.

entries.title: Is celebrated the festival of the Birth of our Lord and Redeemer Jesus Christ
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates is celebrated the festival of the Birth of our Lord and Redeemer Jesus Christ, by the Lady of us all, the holy two-fold virg in Mary, the God-bearer, for ever and ever, Am en. It also remembers the great Saint Dem etrius became a martyr. Salutation to Thy birth O Flower Who blossom edwithout wa.

entries.title: The holy father
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father, the fighter who prayed always, Abraham the anchorite. It also remembers Fa libun, and Kayna, and the appearance of the head of John the Baptist, and Martes, and Mars, and Isaac [the king]. This holy man was a native of the city of Ma nuf, and his parents feared God, and served Christ, and they were exceedingly rich in the possessions of this world.

entries.title: Abba Sanitius
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Abba Sanitius, the sixty-thir d Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. It also remembers the repose of the holy father Abba Pe, the twenty-seventh Archbishop of the city of Alexandria, who was appointed after Saint Tim othy. This father used to teach the people, and he sat for fifteen years and died in peace.

entries.readMore: This father used to teach the people, and he sat for fifteen years and died in peace. After God had chosen this holy father for the office of archbishop, and he had taken his seat upon the Apostolic Throne, Akkaeus , Archbishop of the city of Constantine, wrote to him a letter wherein he spoke and confessed the Holy Trinity, One God, according to the Faith of Saints Cyril and Dioscurus, and he also spoke clearly in it, saying, "It is not right to separate His Divine Nature from His Incarna tion, and after their uni on the benefit of the union must not be cut off." And Abba Peter wrote an answer to his letter wherein he accepted [what he had said] concerning the True Faith, and he sent the letter to him with three learned bishops. When they cam...

entries.title: The great Saint Cyriacus
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the great Saint Cyriacus. This holy man was born in the city of Corinth, and his parents were Orthodox Christians, and they taught him the doctrine of the Orthodox Church. They brought him to Abba Pe, Bishop of the city of Corinth, and they prayed over him and appointed him a read er; now he was the son of this father'sbrother.

entries.title: Saints Epim achus and Azarianus became martyrs
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saints Epim achus and Azarianus became martyrs. It also remembers Jam esand John, Bishops of the country of Persia, became martyrs by the hand of Sapor, the son of Herm az the King of Persia. These menwere from Rome, and certain menaccused them before the governor who had been appointed by Maxim ianus of being believers in Christ.

entries.readMore: Now the Emperor Tiberius Caesar sent [soldiers] to the country of Cappadocia, and they cut off the head of Saint Longinus on the twenty-third da y of the month Ham l e . the head of the holy man and his body were cas tout on the ground, and the soldier who had com efrom the em peror to cut off his head took it and carried the head away with him to Jerusalem and gave it to Pilate the Pontinian. Pilate the governor showed the head to the Jews, who rejoiced and were glad at his death. Pilate commanded his soldiers to bury the head of the holy man in a certain hill, which was outside the city of Jerusalem, and they went and buried the head there.

entries.title: Felix
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father Felix, Archbis hop of the city of Rome. The parents of this holy man were Christians, and they taught him all the doctrine of the Church, and Anastasius, Archbishop of the city of Ro m e, made him a deacon. When Justus was appointed Archbishop of the city of Ro m e, he perceived the intelligence and good disposition of this man, and his fair righte ousness, and he made him a priest.

entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint George the Great, the Alexa ndrian, became a martyr. It also remembers the holy man Abba Nahrew became a martyr. The father of this holy man was a foreigner, and he was of th eme nof Alexandria, and he had no son.

entries.title: Abba Isaac
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father Abba Isaac, the forty-first Archbishop of Alexandria. This father was one of the menof the city of Bu rles , and his parents were exceedingly rich, and they feared God. After very many days they begot this holy man, and they rejoiced over him with very great joy.

entries.title: Fifty nuns
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates fifty nuns, who were pure and holy virgins, together with their mother Sofia, became martyrs. It also remembers a General Council of the Saints took place in the days of Victor, Archbishop of the city of Rome, and in the days of Dem etrius, Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. entries.title: Canitus
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father Canitus, that is to say Marcius, Bishop of the country of Thrace. It also remembers the repose of Abba Daniel, who worked miracles for the King of Persia, and made him to believe on the Nam eof Jesus Christ. This holy man was a native of the city of Sorpeia, and his parents were Christians; he was an ascetic, and a fighter, and he was gentle and good.

entries.readMore: This holy man was a native of the city of Sorpeia, and his parents were Christians; he was an ascetic, and a fighter, and he was gentle and good. He persecuted the followers of Arius continually, and he rebuked them, and cursed them, and called them "infidels"; because of this and through them a very great tribulation cam eupon him. they used to lie in wait for him in the m a rket places, and when he passed they seized him on many occasions, and punished him and beat him, and very often they dragged him about through the city by his feet. he fled from them to a city, which was afar off, and he cam eto the shore of the Red Sea and he dwelt there in a cave for many years, and he lived upon what grew out of the ground.

entries.title: Saint Minas became a martyr
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Minas became a martyr; now the inpretation of his nam eis "believing" and "faithf u l." The father of this holy man was a native of the city of Nakiyos and his nam ewas Eudoxius, and he was a gove rnor and a noblem an. It also remembers the repose of the holy father Abba Minas the Second, the sixty-first Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. Now the mother of Saint Minas had no son.

entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates was consecrated the church of th eblessed Saint 'Abunafer, the dweller in the desert, [ which is built] outside the city of Me sr . It also remembers the repose of the holy father, Abba John, the forty-eighth Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. entries.title: The holy virgins Atrasis and Yona became martyrs
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the holy virgins Atrasis and Yona became martyrs. It also remembers the repose of Philip the apostle, one of the Twelve Apostles. This Saint Atrasis was the daugh of King Iskandrianus who worshipped idols; and he made her a palace, and he established her therein by herself so that she might never see ama n.

entries.title: Anianus
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father Anianus, Arc hbishop of the city of Alexandria, the second from Mark the evangelist. It also remembers took place the consecration of the church of Saint Theodore the general. This holy man was a native of Alexandria and his parents were infidel pagans, and his trade was that of a sandal m a ker.

entries.title: Saint Gregory
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Saint Gregory, the worker of signs and wonders. It also remembers the repose of the holy father Abba Cosm as, the fifty-first Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. This holy man came from the country of Rome, and from his earliest years he was taught philosophy and the True Faith.

He was a true Christian, and he rem embered the end of this world, and the never-ending abidingness of the kingdom of the heavens, and he devoted all his mind to the salvation of his soul. the bishop of that city asked him to help him in the work of his episcopate, but he did not wish for this for he used to flee from th epraises of men; af this he fled to the desert where he fought a good fight. entries.title: The festival of the Four and Twenty Priests of heaven
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the festival of the Four and Twenty Priests of heaven, who are round about the throne of G od, who are priests indeed, and who are beings of the spirit and have no bodies. It also remembers Azkir, the priest of Nagran, and eight and thirty men who were with him became martyrs in the days of the kingdom of Sarabhel, the King of Ham i r. They are exalted above all the saints and the spiritual hosts.

entries.readMore: Now the occupation of his father, and grandfather, and his kinsm enwas that of hunters of wild beasts. One day they went out hunting, accord ing to their custom, and they found two menwith the faces of dogs, and they ate his grandfat her, and they also wanted to eat his father, but the angel of the Lord prevented them from doing so. the angel said unto them, "Touch him not, for from him shall go forth good fruit." Then the angel of the Lord surrounded them with fire, and being in tribul ation, the two Dog-faces cam eto the father of Saint Mercurius, and they bowed low befo re him. straightway God changed their [savage] nature to one of gentleness, and th ey becam elike sheep and went with him to the city.

entries.readMore: This holy man was one of the soldiers of Sakrad, the son of Sapor, King of Persia, and he was a friend of the prince, who loved him exceedingly, and took counsel with him about all his affairs; and for this reason his heart inclined towards Saint James, and he made him worship the sun and fire. When his mother and his wife and his sis heard that he had becom eone with the king in his acts and in his faith, they wrote him a letter, saying, "W hy hast thou forsaken the Faith of our Lord Jesus Christ, and dost worship natural objects which have been created, that is to say, the sun, and the moon, and fire? Know that if thou dost continue to hold the faith of the King of Persia, we shall becom estrangers to thee." When he had read their letter he...

His father's name was Abraham, and he was a son of Levi, the son of Joseph, brother of Simon, and brother of the mother of Stephen, and at the tim eof his birth he called his name Simon. When his father died he wished to becom e a Christian. the angel of the Lord appeared unto him and commanded him go to Abba John, bishop of the city, and he went to him and the bishop revealed to him the m y sy of Christ our God becom ing man.

entries.readMore: This holy man was learned, and excellent, and good, and he tr anslated books of theology, and he was appointed priest in the church of the city of Constantinople. Now a General Council was held in the city of Chalcedon, and this fath er was not pleased with all that took place thereat. When they searched for him to com ebecause of his knowledge, he made a pretence to them that he was sick. When he heard of the tribulation which they had inflicted upon Saint Dioscurus, Archbishop of th ecity of Alexandria, he was exceedingly sorry, and he began to curse their Council be fore his com panions, and friends, and the principal military officers of the Governm ent whom he knew to be Orthodox, and whose works he knew to be good.

entries.readMore: When Nebuchadn ezzar had made the image of gold he commanded his officers and the people of his kingdom to worship it. when certain meninf orm ed against them and told the king th at the saints had refused to worship it, he commanded his soldiers to cast them into a fiery furnace which they had heated seven tim es hot than usual; and they prayed for a long tim ewith their hands stretched out. Then the angel of the Lord went down and made the fire to becom elike a cool wind, and he brought them out, and the fire had neither touched them nor singed the hair of their heads. When Nebuchadnezzar saw this he bowed down and worshipped God, and he honored the Three Children exceedingly.

entries.readMore: The lot of this holy man went forth that he should pr each in the city of Leda, and in the cities of Akorakod. When he cam einto the city of Leda, many of the people thereof believed through Peter his brother; and he had with him Philem on, his disciple, who had a sweet voice, and he was wise, and a good reader, and pious. Andrew the apostle commanded him to go up on the top of certain step sand to read in a sweet voice, and he went up and read in a sweet voice. A nd when the priests of the idols heard of the com ing of Andrew the apostle, they rose up and took their weapons of war and went to the church so that they might hear whether [the Christians] cursed their gods [or not].

entries.readMore: Salutation to Anatolius. This father was a Christian from the country of the East, and he was a merchant and had many possessions. He cam eto Mesr many tim es when traveling, and he dwelt there, and there appeared in him many virtues, and he was merciful to the poor. the report of his excellence and understanding was noised abroad, and the fathers, the bishops, and the el ders, and the doctors agreed concerning him, and they dem ined to make him Archbishop of the city of Alexandria.

entries.readMore: This holy man was from the monasy of the sons of Khadhib, to the north of the province of 'Eshm unayn, in the country of Egypt, and from the city of Tersa; he was the steward of a certain rich man, and was beloved by everybody because of his sincerity and justice. the rich man had a wife who loved Abba Ba'Am i n, and she trusted him greatly. Saint Abba Ba'Ami npondered about the end of the world, and he left the service of that rich man, and he we nt to a monastery in that [place] and became a monk therein. When the rich man heard that Saint Ba'Am inhad left him, and departed, he was exceedingly sorry.

entries.title: Saint Basofreyos became a martyr in the days of the Muslim s
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Basofreyos became a martyr in the days of the Muslim s. It also remembers the repose of Saint Abracius. because of this the Muslim swere wroth with him, and they tortured him severely, and cut off his head with the sword, and he received the crown of martyrdom in the kingdom of the heavens.

entries.readMore: This blessed man became a monk in the church of Gabriel the Archangel, in the city of Mesr , on the banks of the river [Nile], and he fought a great fight therein, and he disputed with the Muslim Shekhs concerning the True Faith, and he made man ifest to them the Godhead of our Lord Christ. because of this the Muslim swere wroth with him, and they tortured him severely, and cut off his head with the sword, and he received the crown of martyrdom in the kingdom of the heavens. Salutation to Befsufyos. This saint was ama nof Upper Egypt, and he put on the garb of the monastic life when he wa stwenty years of age, and he fought a great and a good fight.

entries.title: Saint Simon
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Simon, who was from the city of Upper Manuf, in the north of Egypt, became a martyr in the days of the Muslim s. It also remembers Saint Abba Behur and Abba Minas, the elder, became martyrs. This holy man used to dispute with a certain Muslim Shekh, and he overcam ehim in argum ent.

And the Muslim sseized Simon and tortured him severely, and then they cut off his head with the sword and he received the crown of martyrdom in the kingdom of the heavens. Salutation to Simon. The father of this saint was the King of Athor , and he worshipped graven im ages, the name sof which were Bel and Babel ; and his mother was a Christian.

entries.title: Gregory
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Gregory, Archbishop of Arm enia, who became a martyr, without the shedding of his blood. It also remembers the repose of Saint Luke of th epillar. Tiridates, the King of Arm enia, as we have already said, tortured this saint severely because he would not worshi phis idols, and af he had tortured him he cast him into a dry pit where he remained f orfifteen years; and God was with him.

entries.title: Gideon
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Gideon, one of the judges of Is rael. This just man Gideon was of the tribe of Menasseh and his father's name was Joas; and the angel of the Lord appeared and helped him, and strengthened him, and made hi mjudge over the children of Israel. he commanded him to destroy the altar of idols, and to build an altar to God, and to offer up burnt offerings upon it; and Gideon burnt with fi re the wooden idols, which were broken in pieces, and he did as the Lord commanded.

entries.title: Took place the translation of the body of Saint Luke of the pillar
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates took place the translation of the body of Saint Luke of the pillar, who came from the country of Persia. It also remembers the holy martyrs 'Eusoryays, and 'Euthyos, and Suryan, and Mark, and Bartelel, and Nathaniel, the monk and toiler in the ascetic lif e. He was the capta in of one hundred soldiers, but he gave up his appointm ent, and assum edthe garb of the mona stic life, and took up his abode in one of the monasies of the country of the East; and he dwelt therein many days.

entries.readMore: Salutation to Abba Salam a. Constantine the Just, the great em peror of em perors, translated the body from 'Akrates to the city of Constantinople. When by the W ill of God, Constantine reigned, he took very careful thought for the churches and the works thereof, and he beautif ied with fine decorations all the churches, which were in his kingdom, and especially those in the city of Constantinople, for, that was the seat of his sovereignty. he inspected every work, and he wished [ the builders] to beautif y them with every kind of decoration, and with pearl- stone , and costly stones; and he also made them glorious with spiritu al stones and celestial gem s.

entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the honorable father, Abba J ohn, Bishop of the city of Burles. entries.title: Is celebrated a com mem orative festival of our holy Lady the Virgin Maryam
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates is celebrated a com mem orative festival of our holy Lady the Virgin Maryam, the God-bearer. Salutation to Maryam, who found seed without drinking the dew, and without drinking from the fountain, and who appeared unto Isaac from out of her icon. the Lord chose him to be of the num ber of the Seventy-two disciples, whom He sent out to preach before His Passion, and He called his name Barnabas.

entries.title: The saint and fighter Abba Tim othy
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the saint and fighter Abba Tim othy, the desert monk. It also remembers the repose of David the Just, King and Prophet, and son of Jesse. This holy man had God-loving parents who brought him up pious ly, and taught him the doctrine of the Church; and he wished to adopt the garb of the monastic life, and he went forth from his father and dwelt in a monastery and became a monk therein.
entries.shortSummary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the saint and fighter Abba Tim othy, the desert monk. It also remembers the repose of David the Just, King and Prophet, and son of Jesse. This holy man had God-loving parents who brought him up pious ly, and taught him the doctrine of the Church; and he wished to adopt the garb of the monastic life, and he went forth from his father and dwelt in a monastery and became a monk therein.
commemorations: died the saint and fighter Abba Tim othy, the desert monk.

entries.title: The righteous woman Esther
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the righteous woman Esther, the daughter of the brother of Mardochaeus, the daughter of the br other of Jerem iah the prophet. It also remembers the repose of Abba Pawli who disput ed with Satan in the city of Sam e r, which is called Salonkeya . there was ama n, a Jew, in the city of Sus, whose nam ewas Am inadab, the son of Iyaeru, of the tribe of Benjam in, whom Nebuchadnezzar the king carried off into captivity; and he had a beautiful daughter whose nam ewas Esther .

When her father died she was brought up in the house of Mardochaeus her father'sbrother. One day Arkaskesis the king made ready a great feast, and gathered together with great pom phis governors, and the chief governor Ham a , the Am alekite, who was the most honorable of all his officers. having sum moned Queen Vashti to his table, she refused to com e, and sent back to him a message of de rision; and because of this he was angry with her, and he remove her from her royal rank.

entries.title: John Kam a
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of John Kam a . It also remembers the repose of the Maccabees in the kingdom of the Midianites and Moabites. This holy man was from the city of Sabra, of the country of Mansis in th enorth of Egypt, and his parents were God- fearing believers; and they had no other son, ad th ey wished to rejoice in him in this world.

entries.title: Saint Anastasia became a martyr
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Anastasia became a martyr. her mother reared her piously, and each day and each night she suckled her on Christian doctrine, and at length she brought her into the True Faith, and no one was able to change her belief. When she had grown up and had reached the age for m a rriage her father gave her in m a rriage to a man who was an infide llike himself, and she hated him exceedingly, and had no wish whatsoever to consort with hi m.

entries.title: Saint Absadi the bishop became a martyr
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Absadi the bishop became a martyr. Saint Abba 'Absadi asked the im perial messenger to wait for him one day. Then he prepared the Eu charist, and adm inised to the people the Holy Mysteries, and he commanded them to hold fast to the True Faith, and he em braced them and went forth from them, and delivered his soul into the hand of God.

entries.readMore: When the Emperor Diocletian heard the report of the great Bishops Abba 'Absad iand Abba Hellanicus, of Upper Egypt, and that they were conf irm ing the Christians in the True Faith of our Lord Christ, and were destroying the worship of idols, he sent messengers and had them brought to him, and he tortured them very severely. Saint Abba 'Absadi asked the im perial messenger to wait for him one day. Then he prepared the Eu charist, and adm inised to the people the Holy Mysteries, and he commanded them to hold fast to the True Faith, and he em braced them and went forth from them, and delivered his soul into the hand of God. the messenger took him to Arianus, the governor of the city of Antinoe.

entries.title: Is celebrated the greatest of all festivals
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates is celebrated the greatest of all festivals, the honorable, and great, and holy festival of the Birth of our Lord Christ, of the holy Virgin Mary. When Paguem e n (i.e. the incalary month) hath six [days], in that year the festival of the Nativity shall be on the eight and twentieth da y; but if Paguem enhath five [days] the festival of the Nativity shall be on the nine and twentieth day.

entries.readMore: Now our fathers, the doctors of the Church, are at one in their opi nion that the festival of the Holy Nativity should be celebrated on two days, for the Nativity took place in the last part of the night of the twenty-eighth day, and in the first part of the night of the twenty-ninth day. When Paguem e n (i.e. the incalary month) hath six [days], in that year the festival of the Nativity shall be on the eight and twentieth da y; but if Paguem enhath five [days] the festival of the Nativity shall be on the nine and twentieth day. the [doctors] have ordained and commanded that the festival of the Nativity shall be celebrated two days, f orit is the chief of the holy festivals.

entries.title: Stephen
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Stephen, the Apostle, Archdeacon and Protom artyr, became a martyr. It also remembers Saint Lavendius became a martyr. This holy man, as the Book of the Acts of the Apostle stestifieth, was full of grace, and of the Holy Spirit, and of power, and he used to perform signs and miracles am ong the people.

entries.title: Abel
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Abel, the righteous man, whose blood was shed by violence; and he was the first-born of the dead, whom Cain his brothe rslew. It also remembers Hellanicus, the bishop [of W eshim ], became a martyr. Then when he went out from the Garden, and descended into the low- [lying] land, he continued for one hundred years to weep and to lam ent, because he was stripped of the glory of his honor, and wa sdriven out from his inheritance.

entries.title: The departure of John the apostle and evangelist took place
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the departure of John the apostle and evangelist took place. When the lot went forth that this saint should go to the country of Asia, he was sad and wept, for he knew that the people there were wicked, and heretics, and stiff-necked; but then he received from our Lord power and consolation. he went fort hto the country, which was his lot, and with him was his disciple Prochorus.

entries.title: Saint Eusegnius
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Saint Eusegnius, the martyr. It also remembers the repose of Abba Matthew, the eighty-eighth Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. This holy man was a soldier in the days of the Emperor Constantine, and he served God, and his charities and alm sto the poor and the needy were very many.

entries.title: Saint Abba Sol-Petros
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Saint Abba Sol-Petros, Archbishop of the city of Rome. It also remembers Saint Abba Ephraim. He was appointed in the eleventh year of the reign of the Em pe ror Constantine the Great.

entries.readMore: Because of the strenuousness of his spiritual fi ght, and his virtues, and his devotion to the ascetic life, and his knowledge, and his righte ousness, they appointed him Archbishop of the city of Rome, after the death of Meletianus, Archbishop of Rome, his predecessor. He was appointed in the eleventh year of the reign of the Em pe ror Constantine the Great. being seated on the throne of Peter, the chief of the Apostles, he baptized Constantine with Christian baptism. Now Constantine did not repent, and was not baptized until the eleventh year of his reign, because up till th at time he was occupied in working and fighting against unbelievers, and in killing them.

entries.title: Was consecrated the church of Sain t Abba Macarius
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates was consecrated the church of Sain t Abba Macarius, of the desert of Scete, by the hand of the honorable saint Abba Benjam in, the thirty-eighth Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. It also remembers the repose of the holy father Abba Andronicus, the thirty-seventh Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. This took place af great tribulation had com eupon him through Makokaz , the heretic, the Melchite; and Abba Benjam in fled into Upper Egypt, [and stayed there] till the end of ten years.

entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father Abba Abraham. It also remembers the hol y fathers George, and Nosm es, and Diskres . The father of this saint was a mercif ul man, and he loved the poor.

entries.readMore: The father of this saint was a mercif ul man, and he loved the poor. in his days th ere was a great famine in the land of Egypt, and he gave all his money to the poor; now he was a very rich man. because of his integrity and his trustworthiness, all the crops of all the fields of every village which were round about his city, and all the crops of his own fields were entrusted to his care, and they were all in his hands. When the famine cam ehe gave all the grain which he had by him to the poor and needy.

entries.readMore: Our learned fathers of the Church who were befo re us, the chiefs of the Councils, have laid down the law that believers should fast on this day until the evening, and that they should eat no kind of moist food whatsoev er, and only that kind which is eaten during the Great Fast of Lent. the reason why th efathers ordained that believers should fast on this day until the evening is this: Th eholy Apostles commanded in the Canon, saying, "If the festival of the Birth or the festival of the Baptism f a ll on the Fourth Day of the week, or on the Sixth Day of the week, let them eat in the morning every kind of food which is eaten at Pentecost, for these two festivals are to God, so that men may not im agine that we regale ourselves with the luxuries...

entries.title: Is celebrated the festival of the gl orious angel
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates is celebrated the festival of the gl orious angel, Michael the Archangel. It also remembers God sent Michael the Archangel to Jacob -Israel when he was afraid of his brother Esau, and he delivered him from him, and brought him over the River Jordan. Jacob went to Laban his mother'sbrother, and married two of his daughs Leah and Rachel.

Duam eyosis, a young man of wisdom and understanding, used to go on errands for them in the city, and buy their food for them, and he told them the talk which he heard.

God spoke by the voice of this prophet concerning His Resurrec tion, and concerning the day of retribution, and what would happen to the children of Is rael, and to those who remained am ong the Gentiles; and he made strong the people of Is rael, and rebuked them often. he was the third captain whom Ahab sent with fif tym ento sum mon Elijah to him. Now, fire having com edown from heaven, and burnt up the tw ocaptains who preceded him, and the men who were with them, at the word of Elija hthe prophet, when 'Abdeyu cam eto him he did not behave with arrogance or show himself proud like the two captains who cam ebefore him.

entries.title: Took place the burning of the bones of the blessed Saint George
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates took place the burning of the bones of the blessed Saint George, the fighter, and chief of the martyrs. It also remembers the repose of the holy father, th efighter and ascetic, Abba Jacob, Bishop of the city of Nisibis, the teacher of Saint Ephraim. The saint and fighter was born and brought up in the city of Nisibis, and he was a Syrian; from his early years he adopted the garb of the monastic life.

entries.readMore: The saint and fighter was born and brought up in the city of Nisibis, and he was a Syrian; from his early years he adopted the garb of the monastic life. He wore sackclot hma de of hair, and he fought the fight by night and by day, with fasting, and prayer, and prostr ations, and vigils, and the heat of sum mer, and the cold of winter, and he never took off his apparel of hairy sackcloth. His food was the herbs of the earth, and his drink was rain-water only, and for this reason his body shone, and his soul sent forth much light, and God gave him the gift of prophecy, and the power to work signs and miracles. he used to tell people what was going to happen before it happened, and this power he possessed to a rem a rkable degree.

entries.title: Saint 'Abrokoros the Apostle
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Saint 'Abrokoros the Apostle, whom the Lord chose to be of the num ber of the Seventy-two Apostles, whom our Lord chose and sent forth to preach, and to whom He gave power to heal the sick and to cast out devils. entries.title: Maria
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Maria, and sainted wom a nand spiritual fighter. It also remembers Abba Absa di, of whom it is said that he had in him the virtues of purity, and that because of this God protected him, and caused the mem orial of him to live in all the world. The parents of this saint were Christians of the city of Alexandria, and though certain noblem en of the city of Alexandria desired her, she did not wish [to give herself].

When her father and mother died, all the possessions, which her father had left her, she gave to the poor and needy, with the exception of a very little money. A nd she entered one of the houses for women, which were outside the city of Alexandria, and she assum edthe holy garb of the nun, and she fought a great fight. For twelve years she fought against sleep, and at length having killed [ the desire for] it she never slept duri ng the day; and during the whole of that period of twelve years she fasted continually until the evening, according to the Rule of that nunnery.

entries.readMore: This saint was the son of a widow who was a believer whose nam ewas Akrosia, and who lived in the city of Kiras, in the days of Aranius and Lucius, Kings of the Galatians. the boy grew up, and his mother taught him all the law and doctrine of the Church. When his days were twelve years, he fought a great fight with fasting, and many prayers, and he devoted himself to the ascetic life. He ate nothing which had been cooked by fire, and he never ate meat, but he lived on the produce of the earth, like the Three Children.

entries.title: Saint 'Aksani
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Saint 'Aksani, which is, being inpreted, "stranger." This holy woman was the daughter of rich and noble parent sof the city of Rome, and she was their only child. It also remembers Cyriacus, the martyr and fighter. she fought the spiritual fight from her youth up, and she fasted and prayed frequently by day and by night, and she went to a certain house of virgins in the city of Rome, and devoted herself to them.

entries.title: Saint Abba Longinus
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Saint Abba Longinus, abbot of the monasy of Zegag, which is outside the city of Alexandria. It also remembers the repose of Abba Pawli who was the greatest of all the desert monks, and the equal of the angels who keep vigil. This saint was ama nof Cilicia where he became a monk, and his teacher, who loved Christ, was a righteous monk, who fled from the approbation of men.

At that tim ethe abbot of their monastery die d, and [the monks] wanted to appoint as abbot the elder Abba Lucianus, his teacher. Abba Lucianus took his disciple Longinus, and they went forth from Cilicia, and cam eto a city of Syria, and they took up their abode in a church. our Lord Christ did not wish that their righteousness should be hidden, for a city which is set upon a hill shall not be hidden; and God wrought many signs and wonders by their hands, and they received praise and honor from men.

entries.readMore: He rejected the world in his youth, and went and lived in a cav efor fifteen yeas, and he fought the fight, with long fasting, and prayer, and vigil by night, and prostrations without ceasing; and he never went out from his cell, or ened a cit y, for a period of fifteen years, and he never looked upon the face of a woman. certain evil men who were am ong the followers of Diabolus made a plan against him, and they cau sed a certain harlot to go to the saint, and she went into his cell to him, and she made le wd gestures before him, and invited him to work sin with her. the saint warned her about the fire of Gahannum, and the punishm ent, which is forever, and through him she repented, and fought the spiritual fight and pleased God. after this...

entries.title: The holy apostle Agabus became a martyr
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the holy apostle Agabus became a martyr. It also remembers the repose of Abba Zachariah, whose works were excellent, and whose spiritual fights were very great. The Lord chose this disciple am ong the Seventy-two disciples whom He chose and sent forth to preach before His Passion.

entries.readMore: The Lord chose this disciple am ong the Seventy-two disciples whom He chose and sent forth to preach before His Passion. this man was filled with the Holy Spirit, the Paraclete, f orhe was with the Twelve Apostles in the upper room of Zion. God gave him the gift of prophecy, even as it saith concerning him in the Book of the Acts of the Apostles, that he took the girdle of Paul and bound his own feet therewith, and sai d, "Thus saith the Holy Spirit: The Jews shall take the man who is the owner of this girdle, and shall bind him thus in Jerusalem " ; and this prophecy was fulfilled. Then he preached with the Apostles the preaching of the holy, and life-giving Gospel, and he went into many cities preaching, and teaching, and pointing out the way...

entries.title: The holy father
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father, Abba Akre pinu, the tenth Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. It also remembers the repose of the holy father Abba Besoy, who was called "Pe." This holy man came from Upper Egypt, from the city of 'Akhim . This father was a God- fearing man, and he was holy and pure; and he was a priest in the city of Alexandria.

entries.readMore: This father was a God- fearing man, and he was holy and pure; and he was a priest in the city of Alexandria. when father Claudi an, the archbishop who preceded him, died, the people of the city of Alexandria chose this father and made him archbishop. having been set over the C hurch of God on the throne of Mark the evangelist, he followed a good course of life like the Apostles, and he preached and taught the Christian Faith, and the life-giving Law, and he watched the people with his eyes, and shepherded his flock and helped them. He took no trouble to becom epossessed of one silver dirham, or one dinar, and he only at ebecause he was hungry, and he dressed only to protect his body against cold and heat.

entries.title: They raised up the body of th eholy and honorable Abulidis
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates they raised up the body of th eholy and honorable Abulidis, Archbishop of the city of Rome, the teacher of all the ends of the world. This saint was a noblem an by birth, and a perfect, and learned man, and God chose him to be Archbishop of the city of Rome, af Abba Eucheius, in the first year of the archiepiscopate of Claudian, in the city of Alexandria. this father used to teach his people and protect them against the opinions of the pagans, and strengthen them in the Faith of God.

entries.title: Abba Alexande r
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father Abba Alexande r, the forty-third Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. It also remembers the repose of the holy father, Abba Theodore, the forty-fifth Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. This saint was a monk in De bra Bataron, which is, being inpreted, "Monastery of the Fathers," and, by the W ill of God, he was appointed Archbishop of the city of Alexandria.

entries.readMore: This saint was a monk in De bra Bataron, which is, being inpreted, "Monastery of the Fathers," and, by the W ill of God, he was appointed Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. He was righteous, and pure, and was a learned man, but great tribulation cam eduring the days of his office. For the King of Egypt, who was reigning at that time, appointed his son to rule over th ekingdom, and he plundered the monasteries of the monks of the desert of Scete. Here is an exam ple of his excessive infidelity and wickedness: He went into a monastery to the south of Mesr , and saw a picture of our Lady Mary; now it was decorated, and upon it was a dress of beautiful silk.

entries.title: The holy father
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father, the great one, Abba Barsom a, the Syrian, the father of the monks of the country of Syria. It also remembers Saint Paul the Syrian became a martyr. The parents of this saint were of the people of the city of Sam isot.

Saint Abba Barsom a dwelt in a rock, and he fought a great fight there, and his fam ewa snoised abroad everywhere, and he had many disciples.

entries.title: The holy and honorable Abba Belanteyanos
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the holy and honorable Abba Belanteyanos, Archbishop of the city of Rome, became a martyr. It also remembers the repose of Abba Eulog, the writer and companion of lions. Claudius, the em peror and governor, having risen up against the Em pe ror Philip, and killed him, and began to reign in his stead.

entries.readMore: This honored and learned and wise saint and fighter was appointed Archbishop of the city of Rome, and he sat upon the throne of Peter, the chief of the Apostles, for twelve years in quietness and peace; and he taught the people the beauty of piety, and he preached to them the Faith of righteousness. Claudius, the em peror and governor, having risen up against the Em pe ror Philip, and killed him, and began to reign in his stead. This infidel stirred up great trials and many tribulations for the believers, and he martyred very many people. the Seven Children, who are known to have slept in a cave for many years, fled from him.

entries.title: The glorious angel
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the glorious angel, Michael the Archangel, for on this day God sent him to Sam son the giant, and he lped him until he conquered the Philistines. It also remembers the repose of the holy father Gelasius, the fighter, and devoted man of God. They wanted to kill him, and God gave him power over them, and he blotted them out and in one day killed of them ten hundred with th ejawbone of an ass.

entries.title: The holy and honorable Sergius became a martyr
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the holy and honorable Sergius became a martyr, and with him were his father, and his mother, and his brethren, and many menfr om the city of Athribis. This saint had excellent parents; his father's name was The odore, and his mother's name was Mary. when his days were twenty years, he dem in ed in his heart to die for the Nam eof our Lord Jesus Christ, and he cam eto Cyprianus the governor, and confessed our Lord Jesus Christ before him.

entries.title: Abba Seve rus
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father Abba Seve rus, Archbishop of the city of Antioch, and teacher of the menof the True Faith. It also remembers the repose of the holy father Abba Jacob, the fiftieth Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. The ki nsfolk of this saint came from the city of Rome, and his grandfather was a bishop, whose nam ewas Severus, and he cam ewith the fathers, the Two Hundred Bishops who gathered together in the city of Ephesus.

entries.readMore: The ki nsfolk of this saint came from the city of Rome, and his grandfather was a bishop, whose nam ewas Severus, and he cam ewith the fathers, the Two Hundred Bishops who gathered together in the city of Ephesus. he saw a vision in which, as it were, one said unto him, "The son who shall be born am ong thy offspring shall establish the True Faith, and they shall call his name 'Severus,' like thine own." And when that bishop died, his son begat this saint and called his name Severus. he studied all the philosophy, which was outside (i.e. profane learning), and af this he learned the philosophy of the La wof the Church.

entries.title: The righteous prophet Z achariah
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the righteous prophet Z achariah, who was one of the Twelve Little Prophets. It also remembers the consecrati on of the Church of the Forty Soldiers of the city of Sebastia, who were martyred. This righteous man was of the trib eof Levi and the nam eof his father was Berechiah, who begot him in the land of Gilea d.

God spoke by his tongue many faithful and profitable words, and he prophesied to Iyos edek, and said unto him, "Thou shalt beget a son, and he shall be a priest to God in Jeru salem. " And he took Iyasu and built a sanctuary in Jerusalem, af the Captivity, and was buried therein.

entries.title: The holy and righteous woman Saint Elisabeth
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy and righteous woman Saint Elisabeth, the mother of John the Baptist. This holy woman was from the city of Jerusalem, and the nam eof her mother was Sophia, and nam eof her father was Matat, the son of Levi, and son of Me lki, of the tribe of Aaron, of the tribe of Levi; and he begot three daughs. By kin she was the daughter of the sister of the mother of our Lady Mary, the mother of God in the flesh.

entries.readMore: This saint was from the country of 'Akm im in Upper Egypt; his parents we re Christians and they were tillers of the ground. This saint wished to array himself in the garb of the monk, and to reject this world, and he became a monk in one of the mona sies of the city of 'Akm im ; he used to fast frequently for two days at a time, and he took no thought for his food and drink. after this he departed to the district of Esm unayn, and dwelt there in a monastery for seven and twenty years, leading a life of devotion to God, and fighting the fight; and he never went outside the door of that monastery. when the Muslim sreigned in the land of Egypt, and he heard concerning them, and how they said, "God hath no son of His Nature and Godhead, who sprang...

entries.title: Abba Maruna
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father Abba Maruna, the bishop. It also remembers took place the translation of the bodies of the saints who became martyrs in the country of Persia, in the days of Diocletian, the infidel. when Maruna arrived, the King of Persia paid him very great honor, and he lodged him in one of the palaces of his kingdom.

entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father, Agapetus the bishop. It also remembers [Tim othy, of the city of Gaza, and] Saint Matyas, of the city of Cyprus, became martyrs. This holy father lived in the days of the Em perors Diocletian and Maxim ianus, the deni ers [of Christ].

entries.readMore: This holy father lived in the days of the Em perors Diocletian and Maxim ianus, the deni ers [of Christ]. The parents of this saint were believing Christians, and they taught him the doctrine of the Church and he was made a deacon. af this he left his parents and went to a monastery, and he ministered unto the monks, the holy elders who were in it, and he learned from them good piety, and the strict observance of the ascetic life, and the knowledge of how to fight the fight; and he fasted frequently and he prayed and kept vi gil ceaselessly. he used to feed himself daily on the wasted lupines, af ter he had f a st ed from dawn until sunset.

entries.readMore: These saints believed through Paul, the Apostle, when he was in the city of Phrygia. when the infidels made a festiv al in honor of the idol Artem i s, which they called by the nam eof the star of Venus, these saints gathered together, and went into the house of idols to see them, and to mock at their infidelity and error. when they saw the man sacrif icing to the idols, the flam eof divine love waxed great in their hearts, and they went out of the tem ple, and ened th eholy Church of Christ, and added to their praises and exhortation of our Lord Christ. a certain man of those who were there heard them, and he heard in their talk the house of idols mentioned, and how they mocked at the people, and at their idols.

entries.title: The righteous man Hosea the prophet
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the righteous man Hosea the prophet, and he was also called 'Ozya. It also remembers Saint Sadok and the saints who were with him and who were one hundred and twenty and eight in num ber, became martyrs. This righteous man prophesied in the days of five kings, that is to say, Am os and Uzziah, and Jotham and Ahaz and Hezekiah, Kings of Judah.

entries.readMore: This righteous man prophesied in the days of five kings, that is to say, Am os and Uzziah, and Jotham and Ahaz and Hezekiah, Kings of Judah. he said marvelous things in his prophecy, and he rebuked the children of Israel, and called them children of whorem ongers. he made them to know that God would not turn back His wrath from them, and that He would not have com passion upon them, and that He hath rejected us. he said, "If the num ber of the children of Israel be like the sand of the sea which cannot be counted, only a few of them shall be left." And he prophesied concerning the belief of the Gentiles in God, [saying], "I will call those who are not My people, and I will make them My people, and they shall call Me their God." entries.title: Saint Theodore
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates saint Theodore, the Rom a n, became a martyr. This saint was from the city of 'Astir, and he lived in the days of the in fidel Em perors Maxim usand Masabos, who heard that he did not agree with th em in their infidelity. When they we re tired of torturing him they cut off his head with the sword, and he received the crown of martyrdom in the kingdom of the heavens.

entries.title: Saint Barkosos becam ebishop of the city of Jerusalem
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Barkosos becam ebishop of the city of Jerusalem. It also remembers the holy martyr Iskander . This good father was appointed in the days of Alexander Caesar, and he loved the Christians, and this holy father was perfect in all his works.

entries.readMore: This good father was appointed in the days of Alexander Caesar, and he loved the Christians, and this holy father was perfect in all his works. God having chosen him for the office of Bishop of Jerusalem, he pastured his people we ll and truly, like the Apostles. He had been sitting only a very fewdays when Alexande rdied, and af him reigned Maxim ianus Caesar, who afflicted the Christian people with grievous tribulation, and he slew very many of them, som ebeing bishops. som eof them betook them selves to flight, and forsook their native lands.

entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the holy and blessed Abba Makarawi, the bishop, became a martyr. It also remembers the repose of Gregory, the seer of things hidden, of the city of Roha . This father was of the people of the city of 'Esm un Zarays, in the south of Egypt; he was one of its nobles, and was appointed Bishop of the city of Nakyos.

entries.title: The blessed and holy Abba Cosm as
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the blessed and holy Abba Cosm as, Archbishop of the city of Alexandria; he was the fifty-eighth archbishop of that cit y. It also remembers the repose of Barronyos of Banyos. This father was righteous, and pure, and exceedingly merciful and com passionate and he was learned in the Books of the Church, and in the inpretations thereof.

entries.readMore: This holy man became a monk in the church of Saint Abba John in his youth, and he dwelt there and ministered to the holy elders, and fought a good and strenuous fight for thirty-two years; then he was appointed abbot of the monastery of Saint Abba John. he added to his works of righteousness, and to his ascetic labors, and he fasted and pr ayed and kept vigil continually, from the time he became a monk until he died. When he had held the office of chief priest for twenty years, he shut himself up in the church, and up to the day of his death never saw ama nagain. He died fifteen years after, and duri ng these fifteen years he never ate nor drank, except at the beginning of the Sabbath, and on the First Day of the week.

entries.title: Saint Arianus
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Arianus, the governor of An tinoe, became a martyr. Arianus sorrowed, with a very great sorrow, because of the evil things which he had done to the holy martyrs, and because he had tortured them with great severity and unm ercifully. he rose up straightway, and sm ashed his idols, and believe din our Lord Jesus Christ, and forsook the torturing of those who believed in Christ.

The em peror appointed him governor over one of the countries [tributary] to Rom, and he commanded him to protect the Christians, and to treat them with respect, and to love them. When Alfayanos cam eto that country the people found that he worshipped idols, and he commanded all the people to worship idols; but they rose up and cursed him and his idols. that night forty soldiers made an agreem ent am ong them selves to go to that governor, and to receive the crown of martyrdom at his hands.

entries.title: Saint Isidore

entries.title: Abba Michael
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father Abba Michael, the fifty-sixth Archbishop of the city of Alexander. It also remembers our Lord raised the righteous man Lazarus from the dead, he having been dead for four days; and very many people believed because of this great miracle. This father was perfect in all his good works, and there cam eupon him tribulation and great sorrow dur ing his days of office.

entries.title: Our Lord Jesus Christ cam einto Jerusalem and went about it am id "Hosannas
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates our Lord Jesus Christ cam einto Jerusalem and went about it am id "Hosannas," in order that what each of the prophets in his tim ehad prophesied concerning Him might be fulfilled. It also remembers the repose of the holy father and spiritual man Abba Cyril, Bishop of the city of Jerusalem. Jacob said concerning Judah his son, who is likened unto our Lord Christ, "He shall bind His foal to the olive tree and hi sass'scolt unto the branches of the vine" .

entries.readMore: This holy man was a native of the city, the na meof which is Sebra, and from his youth up he rejected the world, and he wished to adopt the garb of monasticism. He went into the desert of Scete, and became a monk in the church of Saint Abba Macarius, and he contended strenuously in the monastic life, and there appeared in him virtues and good works. When Cosm as, the archbishop who was his predecessor, died, the bishops with one consent agreed to appoint this father arc hbishop, and they appoint ed him Archbishop of Alexandria against his will. When he went fort hfrom his city to go to the desert of Scete, according to his custom, as he was journeying along he arrived at the city where he was born.

Onesiphorus remained thus for a few days watching always the many miracles and changes that were wrought.

entries.title: Constantine
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Constantine, the righteous em peror. The nam eof the father of this holy man was "Kuensta," that is to say, "Green," and his mother's name was 'Eleni . Kuensta reigned over the city of Byzantium, and Maxim ianus reigned over the city of Rome, and Diocletian reigned over the city of Antioch and over Egypt.

Kuensta reigned over the city of Byzantium, and Maxim ianus reigned over the city of Rome, and Diocletian reigned over the city of Antioch and over Egypt. This Keunsta was by race a Greek, and he was a pagan, but he was good in disposition, and there was no evil in him, and he loved to do good, and he was co mpassionate and merciful. he went to the city of Rome, and he saw 'Eleni , and he married her; now she was a Christian, and she conceived this King Constantine.

entries.title: Abba Sylvanus
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father Abba Sylvanus. This blessed man became a monk in his youth under the direction of Saint Abba Macarius in the desert of Scete. He strove in every narrow path, and he fasted frequently for long periods, with prayer and many vigils, and humility and love.

entries.title: Saint Christopher
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Saint Christopher, the martyr, whose face was like the face of a dog. It also remembers Malal'el, the son of Cainan. This holy man came from the country, the people whereof used to eat men and dogs.

entries.readMore: This holy man came from the country, the people whereof used to eat men and dogs. They captured him in battle, and his father became a believer through Saint Matthew the apostle. When they captured him he did not believe the words of the men who made him captive, and he prayed to God, and He opened his tongue, and he spoke as spoke those who had made him captive. he rebuked those who afflicted the Christian peoples, and the captain who was over the soldiers beat him very severely.

entries.title: The saints Victor
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the saints Victor, and Decius, and Irene, and many people, both men, and women, and virgins became martyrs. When Constantine was dead Julian the infidel reigned, and he set up the worship of idols, and he honored their priests, and slew many Christians. the history of these saints was noised abroad, how they worked [destruction] in the houses of the idols, and how they sm ashed the idols.

entries.title: The great prophet
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the great prophet, the righteous man, Ezekiel, the son of Buz, the priest, who was made captive, and carried off to the city of Babylon. It also remembers took place the strife of 'Aflam is, and Theodora, and Arsenius, the monk, and Basilides. When Nebuchadnezzar carried the children of Israel into captivity, the spirit of prophecy descended upon Ezekiel when he was in the land of Babylon, and in prophecy he spoke many marvelous words.

entries.title: Saint Mary
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Saint Mary, the Egyptian, the dweller in the desert. It also remembers our Lord Jesus Christ appear ed to Thom as, the apostle, on the eighth day after His resurrection. This holy woman was a native of Egypt, and she came from the city of Alexandria, and her parents were Christians.

entries.title: The great and honored and righteous man
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the great and honored and righteous man, Joachim, the father of our Lady, the holy Virgin Mary, the God-bearer. It also remembers 'Agabos, and Theodora, the martyrs, and Abba Metruf, the son of Abba Muse of Dabra Lebyon. This righteous man was called by three name s, which are as follows: Joachim, and Yonakir, and Zadok.

entries.title: Became martyrs the three holy women
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates became martyrs the three holy women, that is to say, 'Agali, and 'Erani, and Susnonya. It also remembers one hundred and fifty people were martyred at once by a Persian king. These three holy virgins were natives of the city of Thessalonica, and they served God and our Lord Jesus Christ, for th eir parents were God-fearing folk.
