entries.readMore: This holy man was a native of the count ry of Palestine, and his parents were Christians and righteous folk. having be gotten this holy man they them selves brought him up for five years, and then they gave hi mto a certain righteous elder monk to educate and to train thoroughly as a Christian, and to teach him philosophy, and the doctrine of the Church; and the elder received him and treated him like a son in Christ. he taught him philosophy of every kind, and doctrine, and after this he arrayed him in the garb of the monk, and made him a deacon. he grew up doing good works, and he increased in excellence, and he was continually praising G od, and he read the Scriptures by day and by night.

entries.title: And fighter Abba Isaac
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father and fighter Abba Isaac, the disciple of the great father Abba 'Eblo. This holy man renounced the worl dand all the pleasure and delight thereof, and went up to the desert of Scete, and became a monk; he was the disciple of Abba 'Eblo, and he continued to minister unto him for five and twenty years. he fought so strenuous a fight that at length his whole body dried up, but he retained possession of all his faculties.

entries.title: Simon
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Simon, a native of the country of Arm enia, and Bishop of the country of Persia, became a martyr; and with him were one hundred and fifty others who suffered. This holy man lived in the reign of Sapor, the son of Horm izd, who was called, "puller out of the arm s," because when he conquered another king he bound him in fets, and pulled out his arm s. he inflicted very severe tribulation upon the believers, and he laid forced labor upon them.

entries.title: Two ascetics
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates two ascetics, Abba Iyasu and Abba Yosef, the disciples of Abba Milius in Dabra Kuerasan, became martyrs. It also remembers Dionysias the martyr, the deaconess, who was one of the apostles, and whom the disciples of our Lord Christ appointed. behold the story of their martyrdom, and of their strife, is written in the section for the 28th day of this month, which is the day whereon their spiritual father became a martyr.

entries.title: Abba Maximus
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy and spiritual father Abba Maximus, the fifteenth Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. This father was a man who feared God in all his works, and he was a native of the city of Alexandria. His parents were Orthodox Christians, and he learned to read and write Greek, and studied Greek philosophy, and he had a very thorough and perfect knowledge of the Greek tongue.

entries.readMore: This father was a man who feared God in all his works, and he was a native of the city of Alexandria. His parents were Orthodox Christians, and he learned to read and write Greek, and studied Greek philosophy, and he had a very thorough and perfect knowledge of the Greek tongue. Then he leaned the doctrine of the Church, and the Law of God, and Abba 'Ar okla appointed him deacon over the church of the city of Alexandria. Then Abba Dionysius made him a priest, and he became a devoted and strenuous worker in every mat, which concerned the office to which he had been appointed.

entries.title: Saint 'Antibas
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint 'Antibas, Bishop of the city of Bar'ama, the disciple of John the Evangelist, the proclaimer of the Godhead, became a martyr. It also remembers Saba the martyr. This holy man lived in the days of Domitianus, the son of Vespasianus, who laid waste Jerusalem.

entries.readMore: This holy man lived in the days of Domitianus, the son of Vespasianus, who laid waste Jerusalem. This Caesar destroyed the Jews who were in his dominions so completely, that not a Jew could be found therein, and he killed many royal children also, being afraid for his son, and his kingdom, and himself. he heard that the Christians said that Christ was their God and King, and he was afraid and slew many of the believers, and John the Evangelist, that is to say, "preacher," fled from him. he sent to Jerusalem, and he made his soldiers bring the children of Juda hto the city of Rome, the son of Yosef being bound in fets.

entries.title: Simon
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Simon, a native of the country of Arm enia, and Bishop of the country of Persia, became a martyr; and with him were one hundred and fifty others who suffered. This holy man lived in the reign of Sapor, the son of Horm izd, who was called, "puller out of the arm s," because when he conquered another king he bound him in fets, and pulled out his arm s. he inflicted very severe tribulation upon the believers, and he laid forced labor upon them.

entries.title: Is the festival of the commemoration of our holy Lady
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates is the festival of the commemoration of our holy Lady, the Virgin Mary, the God-bearer, who maketh incession with her Beloved Son on behalf of the children of men, and maketh Him to forgive the sins of those who call upon her name. It also remembers the repose of the holy father Berutawos. Therefore it is meet for us to keep the feast on the day of her commemoration.

entries.readMore: Therefore it is meet for us to keep the feast on the day of her commemoration. Salutation and blessing be to the virginity of the Lady Mary, both internally and externally. This honorable man was one of the learned counselors and philosophers in the city of Athens, and he was one of the num bers of the learned sages of the Council Cham ber. He frequented the com pany of Saint Paul, the Apostle, and they disputed together about the Faith, and it was clear to Berutawos that there was no True Faith except that of our Lord Jesus Christ.

entries.title: Abba Isaac
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father Abba Isaac, from the city of Harin, in the district of Sebko, in the north of Egypt. This holy man had pious parents, and his father's name was Abraham, and his mother's name was Sosna; his mother died when he was a child and left him alone with his father. When he had grown a little, he herded his father's sheep, and his father married another wife.

When he had grown a little, he herded his father's sheep, and his father married another wife. In those days there was a great famine, and his father's wife hated him, and only gave him a little bread, and this holy man used to give his food to the shepherds, and fasted the whole day long until the evening; now at that time he was five years old. When his father knew that his son used to give his food to the shepherds and fast all day until the evening, he went to see him.

entries.title: Saint Sana

It also remembers the repose of the holy father Abba Sanutya, the fifty-fifth Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. entries.title: Job
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Job, the just. It also remembers the repose of the holy father Abba Theodore, the disciple of the holy father Abba Pachom ius, father of the congregation of the spirit. This Job was just, and excellent in his days, and God testifieth concerning him in the Holy Scriptures that there was none so just as he in his days.

entries.readMore: This holy man was one of the Seventy-two disciples whom the Lord chose. He preach ed with the Apostles, and he suffered the sufferings of our Lord, and God, and Redeemer Jesus Christ. he perform edvery many signs and wonders, and power, and grace, and the Holy Spirit, Paraclete, descended upon him, with the Apostles, on the day of the feast of Pentecost, and he became a preacher. He was born in the city of Tarsus, and he was the first man of Tarsus who believed.

It also remembers Sosim a and Abba Noda, slaves of Victor, became martyrs. This holy man was one of the be lieving sons of the pe ople of the city of Alexandria, and he became a monk in his youth in the desert of Scete, in the monasy of Saint Abba Macarius.

entries.title: Jerem iah the prophet
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Jerem iah the prophet, the son of Hilkiah the priest, who was one of the Great Prophets. This just man prophesied in the days of Josiah, the son of Am on, the King of Judah, and Jehoiakim, the son of Zedeki ah. Concerning this pure man God spoke, saying, "I sanctified thee before thou didst com eforth from thy mother'swom b, and I have set thee to be the prophet of the Gentiles, and the rebuker of the people" .

entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy and Apostolic Father, Abba Athanasius, the twentieth Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. when the holy father Abba Alexander died, [the bishops and priests] appointed this father Athanasius Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. this father lived in exile six years, far from his throne in the country of the W est.

entries.title: The pure and holy woman
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the pure and holy woman, the Em press Helena. It also remembers Baydar, and Sylvanus. This holy wom a nbelonged to the people of the city of Roha ; her parents were Christians, and they brought her up carefully and piously, and they taught her the learning of books, and the doctrine of the Church.

She was exceedingly beautiful, and the beauty of her soul exceeded the beauty of her body. In those days Konesta the King of Barntya cam eto the city of Roha , and he heard the story of the virtues of this holy woman, and that she was beautiful in he rperson, and he sought her out and married her, and he begot by her the Emperor Constantin e, the first of the Christian em perors. she brought him up very carefully and she taught him philosophy and science.

entries.title: The righteous and honorable saints
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the righteous and honorable saints, the Three Children, Ananiah, Azariah, and Misael. It also remembers Thecla the martyr, and Paula. entries.title: God sent Michael
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates God sent Michael, the archangel, to Habakkuk, the prophet, in the city of Jerusalem, as he was carrying a mess of lentils, meaning to take it to the reapers in the fields. It also remembers the repose of the glorious father John the Mouth of Gold (i.e. Saint Michael seized Habakkuk by the hair, as he was carrying the food, and he brought him to the city of Babylon forthw ith, and Daniel ate of the food.

entries.readMore: Saint Michael seized Habakkuk by the hair, as he was carrying the food, and he brought him to the city of Babylon forthw ith, and Daniel ate of the food. Saint Michael, the glorious angel, delivered Daniel from the lions' den, and he brought back Habakkuk to the land of Judea, and the food with him, and straightway he stood by the reapers. For this reason the doctors of the Church have commanded us to make a festival in honor of this glorious angel Saint Michael, the archangel, on the twelfth day of the month of. Salutation to Saint Michael.

entries.title: The holy father
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father, the ascetic, and fighter, and wise man Arsenius. This saint was of the menof Rome, and he bel onged to a rich and noble fam ily, and [his parents] taught him the doctrin eof the Church, and they made him a deacon. He was perfect in the wisdom of the Greeks, and in Christian learning, and in the practice and teaching of divine excellences.

He was perfect in the wisdom of the Greeks, and in Christian learning, and in the practice and teaching of divine excellences. when Theodosius the Great was reigning over the country of Rom y a, he sought for a good and wise man to teach his sons Honorius and Arcadius.

entries.title: Abba Pachom ius
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father Abba Pachom ius, the father who was the companion of the beings of the Spirit. It also remembers the martyrdom of Sym machus, of the city of Farma, in the days of Bulam i s, governor of Mesr . This holy man became a monk in his youth with Saint Abba Balam on, and he lived in subm ission to him for many y ears, and he perform edwith great success all the labors of the ascetic lif e.

entries.title: Jesus Sirak
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Jesus Sirak, the son of Sira k, the son of Elezar of Jerusalem, who poured forth wisdom from his heart. He saith in the beginning of his prophecy, "The sands of the sea, and the drops of the rain, and the days of the world, who can num ber them? The height of heaven, the breadth of the earth, and the depth of the abyss, and of wisdom, who can estim ate them?" He speaketh concerning the unity of our Lord Christ with His Father.

entries.title: The great Saint Epiphanius
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the great Saint Epiphanius, Bishop of Cyprus. This holy man came from a village which was near Beth Gabriel, and hi sparents were Jews, and they walked in the Law of Moses; now they were poor, for the father of this saint was a slave, but they were righteous. the father of th is saint died and left him and one daugh, and their mother brought them up in the Law of Moses.

entries.title: The Holy Spirit descended upon the Apostles
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the Holy Spirit descended upon the Apostles, ten days af the Ascension of our Lord Jesus Christ into heaven, even as He prom ised them, saying, "I will send unto you another Paraclete, Who shall com efrom the Father. It also remembers the repose of the holy father Abba Ga'argi, the companion of Abba Abraham. they were filled with the spirit of power, and they all began to speak the tongues of all countries, each a different tongue, according as the Holy Spirit gave them power.

entries.title: The holy fath er and fighter
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy fath er and fighter, Abba Isaac, the priest of the monasteries of Debre Macarius. It also remembers the holy, and mighty, and great, and honorable fighter, the blessed Isidore, became a martyr. This holy man sprang from one of the sm all ham lets about Mesr , and his parents were poor [in the possessions of this world], but rich in good works, and they inherited the Country of the Living.

entries.title: The holy and just man Cale b
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy and just man Cale b, King of Ethiopia. It also remembers the repose of the holy father Abba Am monius, of Dabra Tona. This saint loved and feared God, and he was of the True Faith.

entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Andronicus, the holy apostle. It also remembers ; and straightway Julianus the apostle prepar ed him for burial and laid him in a grave, and he himself died on the following day. Our Lord and Redeemer Jesus Christ chose this saint, who was one of the num bers of the Seventy-two disciples, whom He sent out before His Passion to preach the kingdom of God.

After this the holy apostles laid thei rhands upon him, and they made him Bishop of the city of Niwebyas, and he preached th erein, and converted many of the pagans [Syrians], and brought them into the Faith of our Lord Jesus Christ, they having dwelt in the darkness of infidelity. after this he took Julianus, the apostle, and they went about through many cities, and they preached and baptized innum erable folk.

entries.title: Saint Julian
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Saint Julian, the apostle. This saint was a son of the house of Gabriel, [who was] of the children of Israel and of the tribe of Judah. Our Lord Jesus Christ chose him am ong the num ber of the Seventy-two disciple s, and he received the gift of the Holy Spirit, Paraclete; and he preached with th eapostles, and there cam eupon him, as upon the apostles, great tribulation and persecution.

entries.title: Our Lord Jesus Christ cam eto the land of Egypt
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates our Lord Jesus Christ cam eto the land of Egypt, and He was a child whose days were two years, even as the Holy Gosp el saith. It also remembers , and we should sing on it the words of David the prophet, "God hath wrought signs in the land of Egypt, and wonders in the Field of Zoan" . Our Lord, in the operation of His wisdom, fled before Herod, but it was not th rough fear that He fled.

entries.title: Saint Thom as the apostle and martyr
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Thom as the apostle and martyr, who was called "Didym us," that is to say "Twin," became a martyr, af he had preached in the country of India and Kantara. It also remembers Alphaeus, the martyr, and Zacharias, the anchorite, and Saint 'Arsonwa, the wife of Lucius, the governor. this saint having arrived in the country of Indi a, made himself a slave of a certain governor, whose nam ewas Lucius; now that governor was a friend of the king.

entries.readMore: this saint having arrived in the country of Indi a, made himself a slave of a certain governor, whose nam ewas Lucius; now that governor was a friend of the king. he asked Thom as, saying, "Tell mewhat handicraft thou knowest." And Thom as said unto him, "I am an architect, and I can build tem ples and pa laces. I am also a carpenter and I can make im plem ents for use in the fields, and seats, and other things [in wood]. I am also a physician, and I can heal the sick who are sm itte nwith sores." And when his m a ster heard the words of Thom as, he was pleased exceedingly, and said, "I have gotten a slave who will be of use to the king." And at that tim ethat governor set out to go to the king, and he left Saint Thom as to build in the house.

entries.title: The holy father
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father, Abba J ohn, the thirtieth Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. It also remembers the repose of Lazarus, the just man, the brother of Mary and Martha, having been appointed Bishop of the city of Cyprus, after our Lord rose from the dead. This saint was a Christian, and a good man, and he became a monk in his early years, and he fought a strenuous spiritual fi ght.

entries.title: Is celebrated the festival of the Birth of our Lord and God and Redeemer
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates is celebrated the festival of the Birth of our Lord and God and Redeemer, Jesus Christ; the sons of the Church celebrate it each month, and beg for mercy and forgiveness for their sins. It also remembers the repose of the holy father Abba Simon, of the monasy of Antioch. Salutation to Thy Birth, O our Lord.

entries.readMore: Salutation to Thy Birth, O our Lord. The nam eof the father of this saint was John, and the nam eof his mother was Martha, and through him signs and wonders took place. Thus before his mother conceived him, Saint John the Baptist cam eto her in a dream, and told her about the birth of this John, and he revealed unto her what would happen to him. having been born, and his days being six years, Simon departed to the monaster y of Antioch, and brought himself under the yoke of the monastic life; and he becam ean asce tic and fought a strenuous spiritual fight.

entries.title: The pure and holy father Abba Michael
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the pure and holy father Abba Michael, the sixty-eighth Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. It also remembers the repose of the Apostle Sisticor us, one of the Seventy-two disciples. This father was a learne dma n, and he studied the Books of the Church from his early years, and many of them he knew by heart.

entries.readMore: This father was a learne dma n, and he studied the Books of the Church from his early years, and many of them he knew by heart. his pure soul desired to fight the spiritual fight under the divine rule of the monastic life, and to becom e a soldier of God from his early years. he went to the desert of Scete and dwelt near the monasy of Saint Abba Macarius for many year s, and he was made a priest. after this he went to the city of Sengar of the country of Egypt, and he shut himself up in a cell for a period of forty years, and considerably more, and he fought a gr eat spiritual fight in that cell.

entries.title: Took place the consecration of the chur ch of Laventius
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates took place the consecration of the chur ch of Laventius, in the country of Syria, where many signs and wonders appeared. It also remembers Saint Jo seph, the just, the son of Jacob-Israel, and Asnet his wife. This holy man became a martyr in the city of Tarablos, on the twenty-second day of the month of Ham l e.

entries.readMore: This holy man became a martyr in the city of Tarablos, on the twenty-second day of the month of Ham l e. a certain Christian woman, the wife of a very great noblem an, cam e, and having given much money to the soldiers to be allowed to take his body she ca rried it away, and wrapped it in cloth of gold, which belonged to her husband, and then she laid it in a coffer which was in her house, and she hung a lighted lam pbefore it, and painted a picture of him. Now her husband was a judge, and Diocletian was wroth with him and shut him up in prison in the city of Antioch. the wom a nhaving made ready for burial the body of the saint, entreated it honorably, and wrapped it in cloth of gold.

entries.title: The body of Saint John the Baptis tand the body of Saint Elisha
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the body of Saint John the Baptis tand the body of Saint Elisha, the prophet, the disciple of Elijah, the zealous, appeared in the city of Alexandria. It also remembers Kerlos, and 'Akeltus the martyr, and Questus the monk. Julian having thrice commanded the building of it, it was destroyed.

Julian having thrice commanded the building of it, it was destroyed. the Jews said, "In this pl ace there are som ebodies of Christian elders, and unless thou dost cast them out the Tem ple cannot be [re] built." entries.title: Saint Sanusi
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Sanusi, from the city of Ba lkim, became a martyr. It also remembers John, of the city of 'Arakli, became a martyr. When the governor was tir ed of torturing him he sent him, with many [other] martyrs, to the governor of the city of Antinoe, who tortured him very severely.

One day the angel of God appeared unto Sanusi and said unto him, "Rise up, and go to the governor, and confess thy God, so that thou m a yest receive the crown of martyrdom." entries.title: Abba Jac ob
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father Abba Jac ob, from the East. It also remembers Abba 'Ebsoy, the fighter, became a martyr. This holy confessor becam ean ascetic, and fought the fight of God in one of the monasies of the East, in the days of Constantine, the son of Constantine the Great, and in the days of Julian the infidel, and in the days of W a lentos, the believer.

entries.title: Theodore the monk of the city of Alexandria
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Theodore the monk of the city of Alexandria, became a martyr. It also remembers four martyrs of Mareotis, ad forty other martyrs, and Gebre Christos, the monk. This saint was a believer and a pure man, and he devoted him sel fto the ascetic life, and fought the divine fight, and he dwelt in one of the monasies of the city of Alexandria.

These men made a covenant with Abba 'Abaskiron to shed their blood for the sake of the Nam eof our Lord Jesus Christ.

entries.title: Saint Claudius
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Saint Claudius, the martyr, ama nwith the appearance of the angels, a kinsm a nof kings, and a victor in battle. It also remembers the cons ecration of the church of the Forty soldier- martyrs in the church of our Redeemer Jesu s Christ, in the city of Alexandria, and Kirubaweyanu, the archbishop. Th is saint fought a good fight, and received an incorruptible crown of martyrdom in the kingdom of the heavens.

entries.readMore: Th is saint fought a good fight, and received an incorruptible crown of martyrdom in the kingdom of the heavens. He rejected the glory of this fleeting world, and inherited that which is everlasting and unending, and the heavenly grace. The father of this saint was the brother of Num erianus, Emperor of Rome, and his nam ewas 'Abtelm a wos , and the appearance of this saint was very goodly, like that of Joseph, the son of Jacob-Israel. he was mighty in battle, and he was applauded more than any other young man in Antioch; and he was beloved by everyone because of his strength and goodliness.

entries.title: Is celebrated the commemoration of the glorious angel Michael the Archangel
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates is celebrated the commemoration of the glorious angel Michael the Archangel. It also remembers the death and history of Saint Euphem i a. When Abba Alexander was appointed Archbishop of Alexandria, the righteous Em pe ror Constantine was reigning, and the True Christian Faith spread abroad.

entries.readMore: Now the reason for the celebration of the festival is as follows: There was in the city of Alexandria a great tem ple which Queen Cl eopatra, the daugh of Ptolem y, King of Egypt, had built in the nam eof th estar Zuhal , and they celebrated a festival in its honor on the twelfth day of the month of Sane. there was in that tem ple a great brass idol which was exceedingly awesom e, and its na mewas Zuhal , and they used to offer up many sacrifices to it on the day of the festival; and they continued to celebrate the festival of that idol until the days of the ar chiepiscopate of Archbishop Alexander, that is to say for three hundred years and more. entries.title: John
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father John, Bishop of Jerusalem. This saint became a monk in his early years in the monasy of Saint Abba Hilarion the Great, with the father Abba Epiphanius, and he devoted himself to the as cetic life and fought a great fight. his knowledge and his virtues were noised abroad, and [the bishops and priests] chose him, and made him Bishop of Jerusalem, after he had been appointed Bishop of Cyprus.

entries.title: Became martyrs the saints Abba 'Akra
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates became martyrs the saints Abba 'Akra, and John, and 'Abtelm a, and Philip. This Saint Abba 'Akra was of th eme nof the city of Dam a nhur, in the district of Busir, in the north of Egypt, and west of the river of Mesr ; he had a brother, whose nam ewas Philip, and he was exceedingly rich. they made an agreem ent with their two priests, whose name swere John and 'Abtelm a, and the four of them cam eto the city of Kartes, and confessed our Lord Jesus Christ before the governor.

entries.readMore: This Saint Abba 'Akra was of th eme nof the city of Dam a nhur, in the district of Busir, in the north of Egypt, and west of the river of Mesr ; he had a brother, whose nam ewas Philip, and he was exceedingly rich. they made an agreem ent with their two priests, whose name swere John and 'Abtelm a, and the four of them cam eto the city of Kartes, and confessed our Lord Jesus Christ before the governor. the governor commanded the soldiers to shoot them to death with arrows, and they shot at them, but the arrows never reached them. af this the governor commanded them to cast them into a red-hot oven, and they did so, and they heaped up the fire upon them, but God sent His angel and delivered them from the fire.

entries.title: At a good old age the ascetic father and fighter
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of at a good old age the ascetic father and fighter, of a glorious mem ory, Abba Nafer, who was the first ascetic [to live] in the desert of Upper Egypt. It also remembers the angel of God appeared to Joseph in a dream, and told him to take the Child and His mother, and to return to the land of Israel. Now the history of this saint is related unt ous by Paphnutius, who was moved by the grace of God, and was sent to see the servants of God who lived in the desert.

When this father cam einto the desert he found a well of water, and a palm tree, and he lived in that desert for eight years. one day this saint Paphnutius saw Abba Nafer com ing to him, and he was naked, and the hair of his head and his beard covered his body.

When he heard this, his heart becam ehot like fire with the love of God, and having received the Holy Mysies he departed to the monastery of 'Asnokhat, where he fought a great fight with ceas eless fasting and prayers; and he used to fast for a week at a time. A nd the angel of God appeared unto him, and told him to go to Saint Isidore, to be endued with the garb of the monastic life by him.

entries.title: The new Saint Ge orge
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the martyrdom of the new Saint Ge orge, that is to say, Mazakham, which is, being inpreted, "Com peller," in the days of the Muslim s. the Muslim soppressed him, and seized him, and tortured him very severely. Th en he escaped and fled to the city of Saft Abu-trab, and dwelt therein for three years.

entries.title: Solom on
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Solom on, the king, the son of David, who reigned in Jerusalem, over Judah and Israel; and the nam eof his moth er was Bersabeh. entries.title: Saint Abba Moses the Black became a martyr
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Abba Moses the Black became a martyr. Men have m a rveled at the spiritual fight of this man, for he seized the kingdom of heaven by force, even as the Holy Gospel saith . This saint was strong in body, and a mighty man in all his deeds.

This saint was strong in body, and a mighty man in all his deeds. He ate, he drank, he killed, he fornic ated, and no man could sta nd up before him. It is said of him that he could eat a whole shee p, and drink a skinf ulof wine at a sitting; he was the slave of a man who worshipped the sun, and he himself, on several occasions, lifted up his eyes to the sun and said, "O sun, if thou art God, make meto know it." And he used to say in his heart, "O thou God W hom I know not, make meto know Thee." entries.title: Saint Judah
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Judah, the apostle and martyr, became a martyr; he was the son of Joseph the carpen and was one of the Sevent y-two disciples. It also remembers the repose of the holy father and fighter Abba Peter, the thirty-fourth Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. This saint preached in many cities, and he cam einto the island and preached therein, and built a church there.

entries.readMore: This saint preached in many cities, and he cam einto the island and preached therein, and built a church there. he went to the city of Edessa and healed Abgar, the King of Edessa, of his illness, and he baptized him with Christian baptism. Afte rthis he went to 'Araz and preached therein, and baptized many of the people thereof with Ch ristian baptism. the governor of that city seized him and tortured him with many tortures; he nailed iron cases on his feet, like sandals, and made him run in them for the distance of a stade, and he hung him up, and shot arrows at him, and the saint delivered up hi ssoul.

entries.title: Saint Thaddeus the apostle
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Saint Thaddeus the apostle, who was one of the Twelve Great Apostles. This blessed apostle our Lord chose, and he was of the num ber of the Twelve Apostles. When the Holy Spirit Paraclete had descended upon him, he departed into all the cities, and preached the preaching of the Holy Gosp el, and he turned many of the Jews and pagans to the knowledge of their Creator, and he baptized them with Christian baptism.

entries.readMore: This blessed apostle our Lord chose, and he was of the num ber of the Twelve Apostles. When the Holy Spirit Paraclete had descended upon him, he departed into all the cities, and preached the preaching of the Holy Gosp el, and he turned many of the Jews and pagans to the knowledge of their Creator, and he baptized them with Christian baptism. One day as he was journeying in the country of Syria our Lord Jesus Christ appeared unto him in the form of a young man with a goodly presence, and He said unto him, "Peace be unto thee O My beloved Thaddeus, the apostle! Fear not, I will be with thee." And the apostle said unto Him, "May Thy peace be with m e." And then He gave him the salutation of "Peace," and went up into heaven.

entries.title: The holy father
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father, Cyril, the pillar of the Faith, the lam pof the Holy Church, the twenty-fourth Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. It also remembers the repose of the holy and honorab le father, Abba Coelestinus, Archbishop of the city of Rome, who was the disciple of Yonaknidos, the archbishop, his predecessor. God gave him grace and intelligence to such a degree that when he had once read a book he knew it by heart.

entries.readMore: This saint was brought up by his mother'sbrother, Theophilus, Archbishop of th ecity of Alexandria, his predecessor, and he sent him to the monasy of Saint Abba Macarius, and he learned from him all divine doctrine; in one year he learned by heart a ll the Holy Books. God gave him grace and intelligence to such a degree that when he had once read a book he knew it by heart. then the archbishop handed him over to the honorable father Abba Sarapam on, the bishop, and his wisdom and knowledge increased, and he learned how to do all good and honorable works. when he had finished learning all the good works, Abba Sarapam on sent him to Abba Theophilus, the archbishop, who rejoiced in him with a great joy, and thanked God Who had given him such a son.

entries.title: Saint John
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint John, and Saint Simon, the son of his father'sbrother, from the city of Sarm alas, became martyrs. It also remembers the great saint Abba Isaiah the anchorite, of the desert of Scete. The mother of Saint John was barren, and his father asked our Lord Jesus Christ to give him a son, and he vowed that if He did so, he would make himself a servant of God all the days of his life.

entries.title: Saint Theodore
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Theodore, Bishop of Five Cities, became a martyr. It also remembers Saint Theodore, Bishop of th ecity of Corinth, and three wom enand the two governors, that is to say Lucianus and Dagnanius, who were torturing them, became martyrs. This saint lived when Diocletian the inf idel worshi pped idols, and when he sent out an Edict into all countries and commanded his officers to torture the Christians, and to destroy them by every kind of torture.

entries.title: Saint John
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint John, and Saint Simon, the son of his father'sbrother, from the city of Sarm alas, became martyrs. It also remembers the great saint Abba Isaiah the anchorite, of the desert of Scete. The mother of Saint John was barren, and his father asked our Lord Jesus Christ to give him a son, and he vowed that if He did so, he would make himself a servant of God all the days of his life.

entries.title: Saint Abba Basendius
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Saint Abba Basendius, Bishop of the city of Keft . It also remembers the martyrdom of Saint Am mon, who was from the city of Tuh, on the borders of Buna in the north of Egypt. This saint became a monk in his early years, and he devot ed himself to the ascetic life, and fought a great fight, and learned many of the holy Books by heart, of their num ber being the Book of the Psalm sof David, and the Twelve Minor Prophets.

entries.title: Saint Abba Nob
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Abba Nob, of the city of Nekhisa, on the borders of the lower land in the north of Egypt, became a martyr. It also remembers Abba Takla 'Adonaya, of Debre Libanos, and Tewolde Medhin, and their com panions, became martyrs. The parent sof this saint were holy and pure, and instructed folk; and having gotten this Saint Abba Nob, they reared him in the fear of God.

entries.title: Andronicu sand his wife Athanasia
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Andronicu sand his wife Athanasia, the lovers of God. It also remembers the repose of the blessed man, th efighter, and the worker of wonders, Abba Philip, father of the Council of the holy city of Debre Libanos. They came from the city of Antioch.

entries.readMore: They came from the city of Antioch. They were rich in gold and in silver, and they gave one half of the profit, which they made on their money to the poor; and af a few days they produced two children, a boy and a girl, and they called their name s John and Mary. then Andronicus and Athanasia took counsel together, and agreed to cease to live together as man and wife, and they began to devote their money and them selves to ministering to the sick and needy, that is to say, he ministered to the men, and she ministered to the women; and they continued to do this for twelve years. then their children fell ill of the plague, and they both died on one day.

When he had fought in the war, and returned to the city of Antioch, he found that Diocletian had denied Christ, and had set up the worship of idols. He was able to destroy Diocletia nand to take his kingdom from him, but he meditated upon a heavenly kingdom, which never passeth away; and Diocletian urged him, with many persuasive words, [to worship idol s,] but he would not hearken unto him. af this Justus urged the saint, until Diocletia nwrote the order, and sent it to Justus, and his wife Theoklera, and his son 'Aboli, who is commemorated this day, and he sent them to the country of Egypt, to Herm inius, governor of the city of Alexandria, and he said in his letter, "When Justus, and his wife, and...

entries.readMore: This hol y woman was a native of the city of Manuf, and her parents were rich. When they died she a rrived in her mind at the wise decision to make her house a dwelling place for monks and pilgrim s, and this she carried into effect. she used to receive all those who cam eto her, and she gave them whatsoever they needed, and at length she cam eto the end of her mone y. certain evil men, whose actions were abom inable, gathered together, and they corr upted her mind and induced her to com mit sin, which she used to do continually, instead of doing what was good.

entries.title: The glorious holy father
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the glorious holy father, the ascetic and fighter, Abba Simon of the Pillar. It also remembers the repose of Saint Sophia. This saint came from the island of Sorya.

entries.readMore: This saint came from the island of Sorya. When his days were three years, his father set him to tend his sheep. he used to go to church frequently and hear the Holy Scriptures, and the grace of God moved him, and he went to a certain monastery and became a monk, and devoted himself to the asce tic life and fought the spiritual fight for many years. he used to carry loads of dust and ashes upon his head, and he afflicted his soul with fasting and much prayer, and with prolonged periods of thirst.

entries.title: King Hezekiah
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of King Hezekiah, son of Ahaz, of the seed of David, of the tribe of Judah. It also remembers the saints David and hi sbrethren, and Philip, became martyrs in the city of Sengera, in the north of Egypt. After David, the prophet, there was never appoi nted any king over the children of Israel like unto this saint, f orall the kings of Isr ael worshipped idols, and built altars to them, with the exception of this king.

When he rei gned he broke the idols, and laid waste their altars, and broke in pieces the serpent of bra ss because the children of Israel worshipped it; and because he had done the things which were good God rewarded him by adding greatly to his days. In the fourteenth year of hi skingdom Sanakrem , the King of Fars , cam eand besieged Jerusalem. Now Sanakrem was a very great and most powerful king, and there was no king as strong and mighty in his days, and all the [other] kings were afra id, and were subject unto him.

entries.title: Saint Justa became a martyr
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Justa became a martyr. It also remembers the repose of the holy father and spiritual fighter Abba W isa, the disciple of Abba Sinoda, and he lived under his authority for many years. This spiritual fighter came from the country of Caesarea in Cappadocia, and she inherited from her parents many possessions; but a certain oppressor robbed her and took away all her money, and possessions, and slaves, and handm aidens by bribery.

entries.title: Saint Abba 'Ori became a martyr
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Abba 'Ori became a martyr. It also remembers Pilate, the Archbishop. This saint was priest of Satanuf, and he was of an exceedingly merciful and com passiona te disposition, and was pure in mind and in body, and he saw divine visions frequently.

entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates three hundred people became martyrs with Basilides the martyr, general of the city of Antioch. entries.title: Is celebrated the festival of our Lord Jesus Christ
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates is celebrated the festival of our Lord Jesus Christ, and how His appearance was changed on Mount Tabor, when there were with Hi mthree of His disciples, that is to say, Pe, and James, and John. It also remembers was the martyrdom of Saint Safrira, the daughter of Moses the prophet. our Lord said concerning them, "There stand here those who shall not taste death until they see the Son of God and of man com ing in His glory." This is the glory wherein the Apostles saw Him.

entries.title: Simon Shalusi and John his friend
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Simon Shalusi and John his friend. It also remembers God worked a great miracle in the city of Alexandria, by the hand of Saint Abba Theophilus, Archbishop of the city of Alexandria, brother of Saint Cyril. These saints were in the kingdom of Yostos , the believing em peror, and their parents were rich and honorable; they wished to keep the festival of the Cross, and they departed to Jerusalem to worship at the holy places.

entries.title: Saint Christina
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Saint Christina, the daugh of Rubanos, became a martyr. It also remembers the repose of Saint Marina. Her parents worshipped idols, and she herself offered incen se to them according as her father had taught her.

entries.title: Took place the Ascension of the body of our Lady Mary
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates took place the Ascension of the body of our Lady Mary, the God-bearer, into heaven. After her death the Apostles continue dto be sad and sorrowful, because of her separation from them, and because they were left orphans by her; for our Lord had given them the hope that they would see her in the flesh. David, the prophet, sang a hym nof praise, saying, "And the Queen shall stand at Thy right hand, and her apparel shall be of fine needlework with gold woven through it" .

entries.title: 'Entawos
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates 'Entawos, the 'Am orawi, became a martyr. It also remembers 'Akrates, the brother of Antaeus, became a martyr. Originally this saint was a wicked man, and knew not Christ, and he devoted all his toil and thought to heap up money.

entries.title: The holy father
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the holy father, Abba Alexa nder, Archbishop of the city of Alexandria. This holy father was an honorable man, and much tribulation cam eupon him through the people of Arius. in his day Abba Atha nasius renewed the excom munication of Arius, and he rem oved him, and expelled his followers, from the churches of the city of Alexandria.

entries.title: Is celebrated a festival com mem orating our Lady Mary
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates is celebrated a festival com mem orating our Lady Mary, the God-bearer, who becam ethe salvation of the world, and who make th incession for us at all tim es, and for all those who celebrate her commemoration. Salutation to thee, O Mary. he made for her a table of gold, and vessels of gold and silver, and he commanded a certain elder to teach her; and then he settled her in the palace, with three handm aidens to render her service, and closed the doors of the palace upon her.

entries.title: Three thousand of our Christian brethren in the city of Alexandria became martyrs
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates three thousand of our Christian brethren in the city of Alexandria became martyrs. It also remembers Saint Dam ianus becam eam a rt yr. this took place when the wicked Emperor Marcianus exiled our father Dioscoros to the island of Gagra; and there was rebellion and great tum ult in the city of Alexandria for many years.

entries.title: The head of the monks and father of all the world
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of the head of the monks and father of all the world, Abuna Takla Haim anot. It also remembers the repose of the holy father, the fighter and conqueror, the soldier and apostle of Christ, Thom as, Bishop of the city of Ma r'as, the confessor. The birthplace of this saint was Zara re, in the country of Showa.

entries.readMore: This Saint Andrianus was one of th eim perial officers, and was captain of the young men, and whilst Maxim ianus was torturing the Christians, because they refused to worship idols, Saint Andrianus saw their boldness of heart and patience under suffering, and he said unto the saints, "W hat do ye expect to get that ye endure such sufferings? " And the holy martyrs said unto him, "Our mouths are unable to describe the hoped-for things which await us," and they told him in the words of the Law and the Prophets about Christ. entries.title: Abba Benjam in and Eudoxia became martyrs
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates Abba Benjam in and Eudoxia became martyrs. It also remembers Samuel the prophet, and how God called him in the days of Eli the priest, and how God made him a prophet to the children of Israel. The parents of these saints were believing folk of the city of Sabersir, and they showed affection to pilgrim sand strangers, and they preserved their purity, and devoted them selves to God, and fought the spiritual fight.

Certain menreported to the king that Saint Athanasius had baptized the daugh of 'Antonitos th egeneral, and the king seized him, and Athanasius confessed before him that he was a Christian; and the ki ng tortured him greatly, but not only would he not deny Christ, but he increased his Faith in Him. Then the king commanded the soldiers to cut of fhis head with the sword, and he likewise tortured the two servants Garsim os and Theodotus severely, and he beat them and crucified them. then the soldiers cut of fthe heads of all th ree of them with the sword, and the saints received crowns of martyrdom in the kingdom of the heavens.

This holy apostle was the disciple of Saint John the evangelist, and he was filled with the gr ace of the Life-giving Holy Spirit, whilst he was minising to J ohn the apostle, and he was subject unto him.

entries.readMore: This holy apostle was the disciple of Saint John the evangelist, and he was filled with the gr ace of the Life-giving Holy Spirit, whilst he was minising to J ohn the apostle, and he was subject unto him. He longed to go with Paul the apostle, and John commanded that he should go with him, and he went with him into the preaching. he preached in the Nam eof our Lord Jesus Christ, and he converted many of the Greeks and brought them into the knowledge of our Lord Jesus Christ, and he baptized them with Christian baptism; and he destroyed the tem ples of idols, and rebuilt them as churches of our Lord Jesus Christ. he endured great tribulation, and im prisonm ent, and beati ngs for many days by the infidels.

entries.title: Saint Titus the apostle
entries.summary: The Synaxarium commemorates the repose of Saint Titus the apostle, unto whom Paul the apostle wrote one of his Epistles. The birthplace of this saint was the city of W a rtes, and he was the son of the sis of the governor of the city, and his ki nsfolk were Greeks. from his childhood he studied the learning of the Greeks, and he was exceedingly wise in his understanding, and sincere in his disposition, and his acts were good, and he loved the poor and needy.

John was the most advanced (i.e. the eldest) of them in his days, and Abba Baym on was most advanced in knowledge and wisdom.

entries.readMore: if the month of Paguem encontaineth six days he (i.e. the priest or monk) shall read the following; any if it hath only five days, he shall read it likewise. On this blessed day, O believing, beloved, and Orthodox brethren, it is meet that ye should m a gnify our Lord Jesus Christ and His honorab le Blood, and that we should praise God the Most High, and multiply His praises, and ex alt His highness exceedingly, because He hath given unto us the grace of His goodness and th egreatness of His com passion, which have brought us to this hour, which is the end of th erevolution of the year of mercy of Egypt, and of Ethiopia, and Nubia. we are sound and healthy in our bodies and souls, and we are strong in the True Faith, following our hol y fathers.
